title: What is the useOptimistic hook in React 19?
options: useOptimistic provides optimistic UI updates; shows pending value immediately while async operation completes, then reconciles with actual result. Built into React 19 for form actions
correct_answer: useOptimistic provides optimistic UI updates; shows pending value immediately while async operation completes, then reconciles with actual result. Built into React 19 for form actions

title: What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled forms?
description: Compare controlled vs uncontrolled form patterns in React.
options: Controlled: React state drives input value via value + onChange. Uncontrolled: DOM manages state via ref + defaultValue. Controlled gives more control; uncontrolled is simpler for basic forms
correct_answer: Controlled: React state drives input value via value + onChange. Uncontrolled: DOM manages state via ref + defaultValue. Controlled gives more control; uncontrolled is simpler for basic forms

title: What is the React compiler (React Forget)?
description: How does React Compiler optimize performance?
options: Auto-memoizes components and hooks at compile time; eliminates need for manual useMemo, useCallback, React.memo. Analyzes component code to insert granular memoization automatically
correct_answer: Auto-memoizes components and hooks at compile time; eliminates need for manual useMemo, useCallback, React.memo. Analyzes component code to insert granular memoization automatically

title: Implement a useIntersectionObserver hook.
description: Create a hook that detects element visibility.

title: What is React Server Components architecture?
description: How do RSCs change the React mental model?
options: Server Components run only on the server (zero JS to client), can access databases/files directly, and serialize to a special format. Client Components handle interactivity. The boundary is marked with 'use client' directive
correct_answer: Server Components run only on the server (zero JS to client), can access databases/files directly, and serialize to a special format. Client Components handle interactivity. The boundary is marked with 'use client' directive

title: Implement useWindowSize custom hook.
description: Create a hook that tracks browser window dimensions.

title: What does flushSync do in React?
options: flushSync forces React to flush state updates synchronously inside the callback, bypassing automatic batching. Useful when you need the DOM to update before the next line runs (e.g., scrolling after state change)
correct_answer: flushSync forces React to flush state updates synchronously inside the callback, bypassing automatic batching. Useful when you need the DOM to update before the next line runs (e.g., scrolling after state change)

title: Implement a compound component pattern.
description: Create a compound component for an accordion.

title: What is the difference between useTransition and useDeferredValue?
options: useTransition wraps a state update as low-priority (returns isPending + startTransition); useDeferredValue creates a deferred copy of a value that lags behind the original during urgent updates. Both prevent blocking the UI
correct_answer: useTransition wraps a state update as low-priority (returns isPending + startTransition); useDeferredValue creates a deferred copy of a value that lags behind the original during urgent updates. Both prevent blocking the UI

title: Implement a React infinite scroll component.
description: Create a component that loads more items as user scrolls.

title: What are React 19 Actions?
description: How do Actions work in React 19?
options: Actions are async functions passed to form's action prop or startTransition; React manages pending state, errors, and optimistic updates automatically. useFormStatus provides pending state; useActionState manages form state
correct_answer: Actions are async functions passed to form's action prop or startTransition; React manages pending state, errors, and optimistic updates automatically. useFormStatus provides pending state; useActionState manages form state

title: Implement a useEventListener custom hook.
description: Create a hook that adds and removes event listeners declaratively.

title: What is React's Suspense boundary for data fetching?
description: How does Suspense work with async data?
options: <Suspense fallback={<Spinner/>}> catches promises thrown by child components; shows fallback while data loads. Works with React.lazy, use() hook, and compatible data fetching libraries. Enables streaming SSR
correct_answer: <Suspense fallback={<Spinner/>}> catches promises thrown by child components; shows fallback while data loads. Works with React.lazy, use() hook, and compatible data fetching libraries. Enables streaming SSR

title: Implement useOnlineStatus hook.
description: Create a hook that tracks network connectivity.

title: What does the use() hook do in React 19?
description: How is the use() hook different from other hooks?
options: use() can read promises and context; uniquely can be called inside conditions/loops unlike other hooks. use(promise) suspends the component until resolved; use(context) reads context value. Replaces useContext for reading context
correct_answer: use() can read promises and context; uniquely can be called inside conditions/loops unlike other hooks. use(promise) suspends the component until resolved; use(context) reads context value. Replaces useContext for reading context
